description_long: This object is a modern solar energy infrastructure complex situated on a rectangular grassy plot. It features multiple large solar panel arrays mounted on flat-roofed structures, with a long, covered walkway lined with solar panels running along one side. Three wind turbines are positioned at the rear, complementing the solar arrays for hybrid renewable energy generation. The buildings are arranged in a grid-like pattern, with open courtyards and flat, reflective surfaces, likely for maintenance or operational access. The structures appear to be constructed from metal and glass, with visible support frames and stair access to the walkway. The overall design is clean, functional, and optimized for maximum solar exposure.
description: A modern solar energy complex with multiple solar panel arrays, a long covered walkway, and three wind turbines on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full layout of solar panel arrays, the long covered walkway, and the three wind turbines at the back.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the length of the covered walkway and the arrangement of the solar panels on the buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the three wind turbines and the back of the main building structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side facades of the buildings, the solar panels, and the slope of the terrain.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, facing away from the wind turbines, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted and ground-mounted on flat-roofed buildings and walkway, supported by metal frames (likely aluminum or steel)
attribute_array_configuration: multiple rows and clusters, approximately 5-6 main panel arrays distributed across the buildings and walkway
